extraction_result.cfp_subject: This funding opportunity, RFA-AI-25-006, from NIH/NIAID, supports research on the molecular and immunological mechanisms of combination adjuvants. The goal is to understand how two or more vaccine adjuvants work together to improve the rational design of vaccines by predicting immune profiles. The research should focus on mechanisms of action (MOA) of combination adjuvants that have demonstrated immune modulating activity, aiming for long-term protective immunity against infectious diseases. It encompasses both basic and clinical research in infectious, allergic, and immunologic diseases.

content.title: Explicit requirement: Required Application Instructions
subtitles: It is critical that applicants follow the instructions in the Research (R) Instructions in the How to Apply - Application Guide, except where instructed to do otherwise (in this NOFO or in a Notice from NIH Guide for Grants and Contracts).
Conformance to all requirements (both in the Application Guide and the NOFO) is required and strictly enforced.
Applicants must read and follow all application instructions in the Application Guide as well as any program-specific instructions noted in Section IV.
When the program-specific instructions deviate from those in the Application Guide, follow the program-specific instructions.
Applications that do not comply with these instructions may be delayed or not accepted for review.
All applications are due by 5:00 PM local time of applicant organization.
Applicants are encouraged to apply early to allow adequate time to make any corrections to errors found in the application during the submission process by the due date.

content.title: Table of Contents - Title only
subtitles: Section I. Notice of Funding Opportunity Description
Section IV. Application and Submission Information

content.title: Part 2. Full Text of Announcement - Title only
subtitles: Section I. Notice of Funding Opportunity Description

content.title: Section I. Notice of Funding Opportunity Description - Title only
Section III. Eligibility Information - Title only
Explicit requirement
subtitles: Studies must explore the mechanism(s) of action (MOA) of combination adjuvants that have already been shown individually to be effective in enhancing or modulating immune responses when compared with antigen alone.
At least one adjuvant combination must be proposed for examination, but additional combinations also are allowed.
Applicants should include a strong rationale for combining adjuvants based on what is known about their individual immunostimulatory properties and/or effects in previously studies.

content.title: Explicit requirement
subtitles: Each applicant will set aside a minimum of $50,000 in direct costs during each year of research, beginning in Year 2.
Investigators should not include or propose specific collaborative projects within their application.

content.title: Explicit requirement: Non-responsive Studies
subtitles: Studies on AIDS, HIV, or SIV will not be reviewed.
Projects that do not examine immune mechanisms in animal models will not be reviewed.

Projects that only examine immune mechanisms in standard inbred and germ-free mouse lines without validation and/or further mechanistic examination using more complex model systems (e.g., Collaborative Cross mice, microbial experienced ("dirty") mice, humanized mice, other relevant animal models, in vitro human models, etc.) will not be reviewed.
Projects that use only one adjuvant without combining it with at least one additional adjuvant will not be reviewed.
Clinical trials will not be reviewed.
